name: Atlantis Rising Cayce
description: The "Atlantis Rising Cayce" concept centers around the alleged psychic readings of Edgar Cayce (1877-1945), an American mystic. Cayce, often called the "Sleeping Prophet," purportedly channeled information while in a trance state, including detailed accounts of Atlantis, a technologically advanced civilization said to have existed thousands of years ago. Cayce's readings describe Atlantis as a powerful nation eventually destroyed by its own hubris and misuse of technology, leading to cataclysmic events and submergence. Proponents believe Cayce's readings provide historical evidence for Atlantis's existence and predict its eventual "rising" or rediscovery, specifically citing locations in the Bimini area and the Azores as potential remnants. They interpret geological anomalies and underwater formations as proof of Atlantean structures. Mainstream science generally dismisses the Atlantis Rising Cayce theory. Geologists and archaeologists find no credible evidence of a lost continent of Atlantis matching Cayce's descriptions. They attribute underwater formations to natural geological processes or misinterpretations of existing historical sites. Cayce's readings are considered pseudoscience, lacking empirical verification and relying on subjective interpretations. The theory is often associated with New Age spirituality and alternative history circles, and it is criticized for lacking rigorous scholarly support and promoting unsubstantiated claims.
subtitle: Edgar Cayce's psychic visions of Atlantis

claims.claim: Atlantis existed as a technologically advanced civilization.
claims.evidence: Edgar Cayce's readings detailing Atlantean society, technology (e.g., crystals), and history.
claims.criticism: Lack of archaeological or geological evidence. Cayce's readings are subjective and lack scientific validation.

claims.claim: Atlantis was destroyed by its own misuse of technology and internal conflict.
claims.evidence: Cayce's descriptions of Atlantean crystals being used for destructive purposes, leading to a cataclysm.
claims.criticism: No physical evidence of advanced technology or a cataclysmic event that matches Cayce's timeline. Attributing a civilization's downfall to technology misuse is a common trope, not necessarily evidence.

claims.claim: Remnants of Atlantis will be found in specific locations like Bimini and the Azores.
claims.evidence: The Bimini Road (a submerged rock formation) and geological features in the Azores are cited as potential Atlantean structures.
claims.criticism: The Bimini Road is a natural geological formation. Geological activity in the Azores is volcanic in origin, not indicative of a sunken continent.

keyFigures.name: Edgar Cayce
keyFigures.role: Source of the psychic readings about Atlantis.

keyFigures.name: Charles Berlitz
keyFigures.role: Popularized the Atlantis theory in his books.

keyFigures.name: Kenneth Feder
keyFigures.role: Archaeologist and skeptic who critiques Atlantis claims.

timeline.event: Edgar Cayce gives readings about Atlantis.
Cayce's Atlantis readings gain popularity through books and articles.
Continued interest in Cayce's Atlantis theory, particularly within New Age and alternative history communities.

sources.author: Kenneth Feder
sources.work: Frauds, Myths, and Mysteries: Science and Pseudoscience in Archaeology
sources.title: Kenneth Feder

sources.author: Greg Little
sources.work: Edgar Cayce's Atlantis and Lemuria: The Lost Civilizations in the Light of Modern Discoveries
sources.title: Greg Little

worshipPractices.name: Meditation and Trancework
worshipPractices.description: Followers often engage in meditation and trancework techniques similar to those Cayce purportedly used to access information about Atlantis. This is done to connect with the akashic records or higher consciousness, seeking insights into the Atlantean civilization and its spiritual wisdom.

worshipPractices.name: Crystal Healing and Energy Work
worshipPractices.description: Atlantis Rising Cayce adherents often incorporate crystal healing and energy work into their spiritual practices. This stems from the belief that Atlanteans possessed advanced knowledge of energy manipulation and the healing properties of crystals.

festivals.name: Atlantis Remembrance Day
festivals.timing: Annually, on a date believed to be significant in Atlantean history (often linked to a celestial event).
festivals.description: This festival commemorates the lost civilization of Atlantis and its advanced knowledge. Participants may engage in rituals honoring Atlantean deities, share stories of Atlantis based on Cayce's readings, and meditate on the potential for humanity's spiritual evolution.

festivals.name: Festival of the Law of One
festivals.description: This festival centers around the core Atlantean spiritual principle, the Law of One, emphasizing unity and interconnectedness. Activities often include group meditations, chanting, and discussions focusing on promoting harmony and understanding among individuals and cultures.

cultural.modernLegacy: The Atlantis Rising Cayce concept continues to inspire New Age spiritual practices, particularly those focused on ancient wisdom and personal transformation. It influences modern interpretations of Atlantis in literature, film, and video games, often portraying it as a source of advanced technology and spiritual enlightenment, albeit with a tragic downfall. The concept also fuels interest in psychic abilities, past life regression, and alternative history.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Like Cayce's readings, the Oracle of Delphi was a source of prophesies and guidance, believed to be divinely inspired and offering insights into the past and future, often concerning significant events and civilizations.

Similar to how Atlantis is seen as a source of ancient wisdom and technology, Yggdrasil is the World Tree that connects all realms and contains the knowledge of the universe, acting as a repository of forgotten lore and power.

The Akashic Records are a cosmic library containing the history of every soul and event, mirroring the Atlantean records and technologies described by Cayce as containing the secrets of the past and potential futures.

Avalon, like Atlantis, is a legendary island associated with advanced knowledge and healing, often depicted as a hidden realm accessible only to those who are spiritually attuned, holding the secrets of ancient magic and power.

associations.name: Rebirth
associations.significance: Atlantis rising symbolizes a cyclical return and the potential for societal renewal, mirroring mythological rebirth motifs.

associations.name: Water
associations.significance: Water represents both the destruction and potential re-emergence of Atlantis, a key element in the myth's narrative.

associations.name: Neptune/Poseidon
associations.significance: As the god of the sea, Neptune/Poseidon is linked to Atlantis' fate and the power that could both destroy and raise it.

associations.name: Aqua
associations.significance: Aqua evokes the ocean depths where Atlantis lies, suggesting hidden knowledge and ancient mysteries.

associations.name: Prophecy
associations.significance: Cayce's readings and the idea of Atlantis rising are intrinsically tied to prophecy and foretelling the future.

associations.name: Seven
associations.significance: Seven, often a mystical number, can symbolize the completion of a cycle, aligning with Atlantis' rise after a period of submersion.

associations.name: Phoenix
associations.significance: The phoenix embodies cyclical rebirth, similar to Atlantis rising from the depths after its destruction.

associations.name: Lost Knowledge
associations.significance: The rising of Atlantis represents the recovery of lost knowledge and advanced technology, mirroring the concept of hidden wisdom in various mythologies.

companions.name: Thoth
companions.description: As the Egyptian god of wisdom, writing, and magic, Thoth could provide Cayce with knowledge regarding Atlantean technology and the civilization's downfall. His vast understanding of ancient secrets would be invaluable.

companions.name: Poseidon
companions.description: Poseidon, the Greek god of the sea, has a direct connection to Atlantis as its patron deity according to some accounts. He could offer insights into the Atlantean's mastery of the oceans and the causes of their submersion.

companions.name: Yggdrasil's Roots
companions.description: A metaphorical connection to the Norse World Tree, Yggdrasil, could provide Cayce with access to hidden knowledge and interconnected pathways across different realms and timelines. This would aid in understanding Atlantis' place within a larger cosmic context.

companions.name: Merlin
companions.description: The legendary wizard Merlin, known for his prophecies and mastery of magic, could act as a guide and protector for Cayce. He could help interpret visions and navigate the dangers associated with uncovering forbidden knowledge.

companions.name: Undine
companions.description: A water spirit or nymph, Undine could assist Cayce in accessing underwater locations and deciphering the secrets hidden within Atlantean ruins. Her connection to the element of water would be vital.

extendedContent.title: Edgar Cayce and the Re-Emergence of Atlantis
extendedContent.content:
Edgar Cayce's prophetic readings are the cornerstone of the 'Atlantis Rising Cayce' concept. Unlike Plato's philosophical treatment of Atlantis as a cautionary tale, Cayce presented Atlantis as a historical reality, a vibrant civilization with advanced technology, including crystal-powered energy sources and flying machines. Cayce's readings, delivered between 1923 and 1945, painted a complex picture of Atlantean society, marked by both remarkable achievements and moral decay. He attributed the civilization's eventual demise to the misuse of its power and a growing divide between those who adhered to spiritual principles and those who pursued purely materialistic goals. This internal conflict, combined with geological instability, ultimately led to catastrophic events that submerged the island continent.

Cayce specified various locations for Atlantis, including areas in the Atlantic Ocean near Bimini, the Azores, and the Sargasso Sea. He predicted the discovery of evidence supporting his claims, such as the infamous 'healing crystal' found off Bimini in 1968. While geologists have since attributed these formations to natural geological processes, Cayce's followers interpret them as remnants of ancient Atlantean structures. The ongoing search for tangible proof continues to fuel the fascination with Cayce's Atlantis and its potential re-emergence, either physically or spiritually, as a force for positive change in the world.

Cayce's interpretations added a unique spiritual dimension to the Atlantis myth, connecting the civilization's downfall to humanity's own struggles with technological advancement and ethical responsibility. His readings emphasized the importance of spiritual growth and the potential for Atlantis to rise again, not necessarily as a physical landmass, but as a symbol of humanity's capacity for both great achievement and devastating self-destruction. This idea has resonated with those seeking alternative interpretations of history and a more spiritually grounded future.

extendedContent.title: Atlantean Technology and Societal Structure According to Cayce
extendedContent.content:
Cayce's readings described Atlantis as a society with advanced technology far surpassing our own in some respects. He claimed they harnessed energy from crystals, utilizing them for power generation, healing, and even levitation. These crystals, particularly the 'Tuaoi Stone,' were central to Atlantean society, providing clean energy and enabling remarkable technological feats. Cayce detailed the use of 'airships' and other advanced vehicles, suggesting a sophisticated understanding of aerodynamics and propulsion. He also spoke of advanced medical techniques and healing modalities, including the use of sound and light therapies.

Atlantean society, according to Cayce, was highly stratified, with distinct social classes and specialized roles. He described a ruling elite, often referred to as the 'Sons of the Law of One,' who adhered to spiritual principles and sought to guide the civilization towards enlightenment. However, over time, a faction known as the 'Sons of Belial' gained prominence, prioritizing material pursuits and exploiting the Atlantean technology for personal gain and power. This growing disparity and conflict between these two factions contributed to the civilization's eventual downfall.

Cayce's descriptions of Atlantean technology and society, while lacking scientific validation, have significantly influenced New Age beliefs and alternative theories about ancient civilizations. His emphasis on crystal energy and the spiritual dimension of technology has resonated with individuals seeking sustainable and holistic approaches to energy and healing. The concept of the 'Sons of the Law of One' and 'Sons of Belial' serves as a moral allegory, highlighting the potential consequences of unchecked technological advancement and the importance of ethical considerations in scientific progress.

extendedContent.title: The Cataclysm and the Fate of the Atlanteans
extendedContent.content:
According to Cayce's readings, Atlantis suffered a series of catastrophic events that ultimately led to its submersion. He described a period of increasing geological instability, marked by earthquakes and volcanic eruptions. The misuse of Atlantean technology, particularly the crystal-powered energy sources, is said to have exacerbated these natural disasters, triggering a chain reaction that culminated in the continent's destruction. Cayce identified three major cataclysms, each contributing to the gradual fragmentation and sinking of Atlantis. The final cataclysm, occurring around 10,000 BC, resulted in the complete submergence of the remaining landmass.

Cayce claimed that some Atlanteans foresaw the impending disaster and sought to preserve their knowledge and culture. He described the migration of Atlantean refugees to various parts of the world, including Egypt, Central America, and the Pyrenees Mountains. These refugees are said to have brought with them their advanced knowledge and skills, influencing the development of these subsequent civilizations. Cayce identified specific individuals, such as the priest Ra-Ta, who played a crucial role in transmitting Atlantean wisdom to ancient Egypt.

The idea of Atlantean refugees influencing other civilizations has become a popular theme in alternative history and esoteric traditions. While mainstream historians reject these claims, they continue to fuel the fascination with Atlantis and its potential legacy. Cayce's readings offer a compelling narrative of a lost civilization that left its mark on the world, both through its material achievements and its spiritual teachings. The cataclysm serves as a cautionary tale, reminding us of the potential consequences of technological hubris and the importance of respecting the natural world.

extendedContent.title: The Legacy of Atlantis in Cayce's Readings and Beyond
extendedContent.content:
The 'Atlantis Rising Cayce' concept extends beyond a simple recounting of a lost civilization. Cayce's readings suggest that Atlantis's influence continues to resonate in our present world, shaping our spiritual, technological, and cultural development. He believed that many individuals are reincarnations of Atlanteans, carrying with them the memories and karmic debts of that ancient civilization. These individuals, according to Cayce, are drawn to the study of Atlantis and possess a natural affinity for the technologies and spiritual practices of that era.

Cayce's predictions about the rediscovery of Atlantean artifacts and the re-emergence of Atlantean consciousness have inspired numerous researchers, authors, and spiritual seekers. His work has contributed to the development of New Age beliefs, particularly those centered on crystal energy, reincarnation, and the interconnectedness of all things. The Association for Research and Enlightenment (ARE), founded by Cayce, continues to promote his teachings and conduct research related to his readings, including investigations into potential Atlantean sites.

The legacy of Atlantis in Cayce's readings is multifaceted. It serves as a reminder of the potential for human greatness and the dangers of unchecked ambition. It also offers a vision of a future where humanity can learn from the mistakes of the past and create a more harmonious and sustainable world. Whether Atlantis is viewed as a historical reality, a metaphorical allegory, or a spiritual archetype, its enduring presence in our collective consciousness underscores its profound and lasting impact.
